title: The Interplay of Freedom and Responsibility in Ethical Theory
content: The ethical consequences are demanding. For Sartre,freedomandresponsibilityare inseparable:freedomis not just the ability to act, but theresponsibilityfor the consequences of those actions. To deny yourfreedom- to claim you "had no choice," to hide behind habit, social pressure, or role - is what Sartre calls bad faith.
